name: Larry Carlton
abstract: Larry Eugene Carlton (born March 2, 1948) is an American guitarist who built his career as a studio musician in the 1970s and 1980s for acts including Steely Dan and Joni Mitchell. One of the most sought-after guitarists of his era, Carlton has participated in thousands of recording sessions, recorded on hundreds of albums in many genres…

concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Bernard Purdie
about_quotes.evidence: Larry Carlton, Paul Griffin, Chuck Rainey. I mean you’re talking about the cream of the crop of musicians who could play anything but played what was necessary for what was asked of them at the time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bernard Purdie interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Gap Mangione
about_quotes.evidence: But then again listen to Grant Geissman or Larry Carlton or Robben Ford — all wonderful players, and anybody who ever sees them play would know that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gap Mangione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rochester, New York, January 11, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Buckingham,
about_quotes.evidence: Some guys got so busy, especially in LA, like Larry Carlton, they started charging triple scale... And again, like Larry can play anything.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Buckingham, interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 25, 2019 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Caramanica
about_quotes.evidence: it’d be people like Larry Carlton and, you know, all these famous studio musicians are there
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Caramanica interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, July 27, 2011

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Bernhardt
about_quotes.evidence: I remember George Wadenius came in and tried to play Larry Carlton’s solo on “Deacon Blues”
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Bernhardt interviewed by Monk Rowe, Bearsville, New York, January 3, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Hey
about_quotes.evidence: Probably maybe Larry Carlton or Greg Parker depending on what the song called for.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Hey interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 2, 2023
